name: -r
--recursive
options.description: Read all files under each directory, recursively

name: -l
--files-with-matches
options.description: Print only names of files with matching lines

name: -L
--files-without-match
options.description: Print only names of files with no matching lines

name: -n
--line-number
options.description: Print line number with output lines

name: -c
--count
options.description: Print only a count of matching lines per file

name: -i
--ignore-case
options.description: Ignore case distinctions in patterns and data

name: -v
--invert-match
options.description: Select non-matching lines

name: -w
--word-regexp
options.description: Match only whole words

name: -x
--line-regexp
options.description: Match only whole lines

name: -E
--extended-regexp
options.description: Use extended regular expressions

name: -P
--perl-regexp
options.description: Use Perl-compatible regular expressions

name: -F
--fixed-strings
options.description: Interpret patterns as fixed strings, not regular expressions

name: -A
--after-context
options.description: Print NUM lines of trailing context after matching lines

name: -B
--before-context
options.description: Print NUM lines of leading context before matching lines

name: -C
--context
options.description: Print NUM lines of output context

name: -m
--max-count
options.description: Stop after NUM selected lines

name: -H
--with-filename
options.description: Print file name with output lines

name: -h
--no-filename
options.description: Suppress the file name prefix on output

name: --include
options.description: Search only files whose name matches the given glob pattern

name: --exclude
options.description: Skip files whose name matches the given glob pattern

name: --exclude-dir
options.description: Skip directories whose name matches the given pattern

name: -o
--only-matching
options.description: Print only the matched parts of a matching line

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Suppress all normal output

name: -s
--no-messages
options.description: Suppress error messages about nonexistent or unreadable files

name: --color
options.description: Use markers to highlight the matching strings

name: -e
--regexp
options.description: Use PATTERN for matching

name: -f
--file
options.description: Obtain patterns from FILE

name: -R
--dereference-recursive
options.description: Read all files recursively, following all symlinks

name: -Z
--null
options.description: Print a zero byte after the file name
